tagline: A time-travel gang warfare system where leadership succession, temporal paradox, and loyalty hierarchies determine outcomes across competing timelines.
visualizationReason: Tokyo Revengers is a temporal loop combat system where gang hierarchies, time-travel triggers, and divergent-future stakes create competing causal branches — the timeline renderer maps how each timeline divergence reshapes gang power structure.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: If I can't save her, I'll just have to change the future.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_OP]: TIMELINE NAVIGATION ACTIVE — PARADOX PROTOCOLS ENGAGED

introductionSummary: Tokyo Revengers operates as a gang-warfare temporal loop system: Takemichi's time-leap ability creates competing causal branches where gang leadership succession determines outcomes. The timeline captures how different Toman leadership outcomes create radically different futures.

aiInsights.casual: Tokyo Revengers is about a guy who can time travel and uses it to become the leader of a gang to save his girlfriend from being killed by a yakuza boss.
aiInsights.deep: The universe's power system is a leadership succession market: gang power is determined by who controls the Tokyo Manji Gang, and Takemichi's time-leap lets him explore different leadership succession paths before committing to the one where everyone survives.

powerSystem.name: Time Leap
powerSystem.subtitle: Emotional Time Travel
powerSystem.loreDesc: Takemichi can travel back in time by gripping someone's hand and experiencing their future death — a death-triggered leap that doesn't require any technology.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A death-triggered temporal jump mechanism that sends the traveler back approximately 12 years, limited by emotional connection to the target of the leap.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Takemichi's first leap back to middle school, discovering he can change the future.

powerSystem.name: Gang Hierarchy
powerSystem.subtitle: Tokyo Manji Kai Power Structure
powerSystem.loreDesc: Toman is organized into divisional command structures with captains, vice-captains, and executives — loyalty to Mikey determines the entire power structure.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A hierarchical loyalty graph where Mikey's decisions cascade through the entire organization — changing one node changes the whole graph.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Mikey expelling Takemichi from Toman to protect him — a loyalty sacrifice that demonstrates the depth of their bond.

powerSystem.name: Kiyoshi's Black Dragons
powerSystem.subtitle: Pre-Toman Gang Economy
powerSystem.loreDesc: The Black Dragons were the dominant gang before Toman — their structure and Kiyoshi's succession crisis created the conditions for Toman's rise.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A predecessor gang market whose dissolution created the power vacuum Toman filled.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Kiyoshi becoming the founder of Toman after losing to Mikey — a respect-based succession.

rankings.topTierLore: Mikey and the gang commanders whose decisions determine gang warfare outcomes across the entire city.
rankings.topTierSystem: Top-tier decision nodes whose leadership choices cascade through the entire gang network.

tiers.name: Executive
tiers.loreDesc: Toman executives who control major divisions and have direct loyalty to Mikey.
tiers.systemDesc: Major network nodes with direct Mikey loyalty edges.

tiers.name: Captain
tiers.loreDesc: Divisional captains who lead individual units within Toman.
tiers.systemDesc: Medium-capability network nodes with divisional authority.

tiers.name: Member
tiers.loreDesc: Standard gang members who form the base of the organizational pyramid.
tiers.systemDesc: Standard network nodes with minimal decision authority.

factions.name: Tokyo Manji Kai (Toman)
factions.loreDesc: Mikey's gang — the most powerful in Tokyo, built on personal loyalty and a no-drugs policy.
factions.systemDesc: A loyalty-based gang network with Mikey at the center — the strongest gang by social capital even when outnumbered.

factions.name: Kanto Management
factions.loreDesc: The yakuza organization that controls gang operations in the Kanto region — functionally the gang economy's institutional regulator.
factions.systemDesc: A institutional authority that monetizes gang activity through protection arrangements.

factions.name: Tenjiku
factions.loreDesc: The rival gang formed by Kisaki to destroy Toman from within — the most dangerous external threat.
factions.systemDesc: A strategic weapon gang built specifically to counter Toman's organizational structure.

rules.name: Time Leap Requires Emotional Connection
rules.loreDesc: Takemichi can only leap by experiencing someone's future death through physical contact — he can't leap arbitrarily.
rules.loreConsequence: He's limited to changing outcomes for people he can physically connect with in the past.
rules.systemEquivalent: A connection-gated temporal mechanism that requires direct emotional handshake to activate.

rules.name: Future Changes Persist Across Leaps
rules.loreDesc: Each successful timeline change becomes permanent — Takemichi is building a new timeline, not temporarily visiting an alternate branch.
rules.loreConsequence: Every change compounds — he can accidentally create worse futures while trying to fix bad ones.
rules.systemEquivalent: A write-through temporal cache: every committed change persists.

rules.name: Mikey's Death Creates the Worst Future
rules.loreDesc: Every timeline where Mikey dies results in the worst possible outcome — his death triggers a cascade that destroys everyone he was protecting.
rules.loreConsequence: Keeping Mikey alive is the primary optimization target for all timeline changes.
rules.systemEquivalent: A critical node whose failure cascades into total system collapse.

characters.name: Takemichi Hanagaki
characters.title: Time Leaper / Toman Member
characters.loreBio: A loser in his late 20s who discovers he can time travel to his middle school self — uses it to save his girlfriend Hina from being killed by the yakuza.
characters.systemBio: A temporal arbitrage node whose only value is his ability to travel in time — he has no combat capability and no social status, making his value entirely dependent on future knowledge.
characters.signatureMoment: First leap back to save Hina from being killed by the yakuza — the inciting event of the entire series.
characters.keyAbility: Time Leap / Emotional Connection Trigger / Determination

characters.name: Mikey (Manjiro Sano)
characters.title: Toman Commander / Invincible Mikey
characters.loreBio: The leader of Toman who is known as 'invincible Mikey' — a martial arts prodigy who can defeat anyone, but who carries a dark streak that emerges when he loses people he loves.
characters.systemBio: The critical node of the entire Toman network: his decisions cascade through the entire organization, and his death or corruption triggers total system failure.
characters.signatureMoment: Going 'dark' after Draken's death and nearly killing everyone in his path — demonstrating that his power is only held in check by his friendships.
characters.keyAbility: Invincible Martial Arts / Dark Impulse / Leadership

characters.name: Ken Ryuguji (Draken)
characters.title: Vice-Commander
characters.loreBio: Mikey's right-hand man and the most reliable fighter in Toman — his death triggers Mikey's dark transformation in multiple timelines.
characters.systemBio: A stabilizing node in the Toman network: Draken's presence moderates Mikey's decisions and prevents the dark impulse from taking over.
characters.signatureMoment: Taking a katana meant for Mikey and surviving multiple times — demonstrating his role as Mikey's shield.
characters.keyAbility: Martial Arts Master / Organizational Leadership / Mikey's Moderator

characters.name: Kisaki Tetta
characters.title: Toman Executive / Dark Strategist
characters.loreBio: The intelligent but sociopathic strategist who joined Toman with a secret goal: getting close to Mikey to eventually destroy everything he loves.
characters.systemBio: A adversarial node operating within the Toman network, pursuing an undisclosed optimization goal that requires Toman's destruction rather than its preservation.
characters.signatureMoment: Being revealed as the mastermind behind multiple timeline disasters — he was manipulating events from the beginning.
characters.keyAbility: Strategic Manipulation / Information Control / Social Engineering

relationships: Takemichi's loyalty to Mikey drives most of his timeline interventions — he keeps trying to save Mikey even when it costs him everything.
A loyalty link that persists across timelines — Takemichi's investment in Mikey's survival is the primary optimization target.
Mikey's best friend and the only person who can moderate his dark impulses — Draken's death triggers Mikey's collapse.
A stabilizing edge that prevents Mikey's critical node failure — Draken's presence is the system's failure prevention mechanism.
Kisaki secretly hates Mikey and has been engineering his downfall from the moment he joined Toman — a mole operating at the highest level.
A deception link where the adversarial node has achieved maximum proximity to the critical node, enabling maximum cascade damage.
Draken becomes one of Takemichi's closest allies and the person who vouches for him in Toman — his respect is hard-earned.
A trust-gain edge that forms through demonstrated loyalty in multiple timeline iterations.

counterplay: Takemichi's timeline changes can undo Kisaki's carefully engineered events — but Kisaki learns from each timeline too.
Kisaki is smart enough to adapt his plans when timeline changes occur, making Takemichi's knowledge less valuable over time.
An adaptive adversary problem: each Takemichi intervention provides information to Kisaki about what changes work.
Mikey's dark impulse is suppressed by his friendships — if Draken and Takemichi are present, he can resist it.
Mikey's 'dark mode' is triggered by loss and grief — his friends are his only defense against his own worst impulses.
A emotional-regulation edge: the critical node's failure mode is suppressed by social connection edges.

causalEvents: Hina is killed by the yakuza in the original timeline
Takemichi is motivated to time travel and change this outcome
Series start — the inciting temporal event
Hina's death is the emotional trigger for Takemichi's first leap, establishing the rules of the time-leap system.
A critical-value-node failure event that activates the temporal arbitrage mechanism.
Draken's death triggers Mikey's dark impulse
Mikey becomes the primary antagonist in the final timeline, killing his own gang members
Mikey's dark transformation is a cascade failure triggered by the loss of his stabilizing moderating edges.
A critical node failure cascade: removing Draken triggers Mikey's failure mode, which destroys the entire Toman network.

anomalies: Kisaki appears to have some awareness of timeline changes — possibly through his own time-leap ability or access to information from parallel timelines.
An information arbitrage node that may have parallel timeline access, creating an adversarial temporal advantage.
If Kisaki has time-leap capability, Takemichi is not the only temporal actor in the system.
Takemichi's assumed sole temporal actor status
Mikey's martial arts talent is so overwhelming that he can defeat anyone — but this same talent means he has no one who can stop him when he goes dark.
An over-optimized capability node with no external constraint: Mikey's maximum power means there's nothing outside himself that can regulate his decisions.
Mikey is simultaneously the most valuable and most dangerous node in the entire system.
External constraint assumption — Mikey has no effective external check on his decisions

systemQuestions: Why is Takemichi the only one who can time travel?
The trigger mechanism (experiencing someone's death through hand contact) is specifically activated by Takemichi's emotional connection to Hina — no one else has been shown with this ability.
Can Takemichi change everything or just specific events?
He can only change events he physically intervenes on — each leap requires him to be present in the past and take specific actions.
What makes Mikey's dark transformation irreversible?
The dark transformation is triggered by the loss of all his moderating social connections — once Draken and Takemichi are both gone, nothing prevents it.

hero: I can travel through time — I just need to find the right moment.
Takemichi has no power except his willingness to keep trying — every victory is earned through determination, not strength.
